posts.name: Kevin Patrick
posts.cooked:
2020 BORDEAUX UGCB -- THE 3RD STRAIGHT GREAT VINTAGE? POSSIBLY - Washington DC (1/24/2023)

I attended the Total Wine Tour de Bordeaux in Washington DC in their partnership with the Union de Grands Crus des Bordeaux revealing the 2020 vintage. I am a skeptic of hype around young vintages but came away duly impressed with the 2020 vintage. For many wines I took no notes but will include them in the closing discussion as either Stellar or Mildly Disappointing. Overall I came away exceedingly impressed with the vintage for its elegance and approachability. How it will stack up in the years to come is impossible for me to predict but I left supremely optimistic.

UGCB 2020 Bordeaux Tasting

These were tasted in 2oz pours over several hours with ample wine and opportunity to re-taste most every wine.

  • 2020 Château Pavie Macquin - France, Bordeaux, Libournais, St. Émilion Grand Cru
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    Soft nose of lavender
    Smooth on the mouthfeel. Savory. Elegant blue fruits with some cassis. Easy drinking.
    Stellar wine
  • 2020 Château Larcis Ducasse - France, Bordeaux, Libournais, St. Émilion Grand Cru
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    Brightly aromatic
    Smooth mouthfeel, raspberries and some blackberry notes, gentle grippiness
    Savory finish
  • 2020 Château Le Gay Pomerol - France, Bordeaux, Libournais, Pomerol
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    Aromatic nose of rose petals;
    rich mineral profile on the palate; great depth of flavor, raspberry, dark cherry, elegant.
    Long finish
    A top wine
    This and Duhart Milon were my top wines on the night.
  • 2020 Château La Dominique - France, Bordeaux, Libournais, St. Émilion Grand Cru
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    Earthy nose, softly aromatic
    Rich mouthfeel, tasty dark cherries with some spice
    Savory finish
  • 2020 Château Clerc Milon - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    Rustic nose; barnyard
    Slight grippiness on palate; currants, red fruits, graphite, iron, complex
    Full bodied finish.
    Really nice Bordeaux
  • 2020 Château Lynch-Moussas - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    Opulent nose; rustic red cherries
    Gentle grippiness on the palate; rich red fruit flavors and bramble; hint of tobacco; great structure though lacking some finesse
    Tasty wine
  • 2020 Château Duhart-Milon - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    Delicious
    72% Cabernet, 28% Merlot
    Yeasty nose of black currants
    Elegant on the mouthfeel
    Black raspberry notes balanced delicately against soft tannins.
    Nice medium+ length finish.
    Drank too easy
    Tied with Le Gay for wine of the night.
  • 2020 Château Smith Haut Lafitte - France, Bordeaux, Graves, Pessac-Léognan
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    Dark inky in color
    Rich, concentrated nose
    Softly tannic on the palate, blackberries and dark fruits, Smoothed out once the wine settled
    California Cabernet/Mike Smith (Myriad) feel but it's a Bordeaux.
  • 2020 Château Desmirail - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Margaux
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    50% Cab Sauvignon/40% Merlot/10% Cabernet Franc
    Gentle nose of rustic blackberries
    Smooth easy drinker; soft dark fruit
    Crowd pleaser while still being a Bordeaux
    Tasty but simple
    EMH Blackcat of Bordeaux
  • 2020 Château Kirwan - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Margaux
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    More muted nose (buy it's late) picking up some dark fruits.
    Velvet glove on the mouthfeel; richly fruited, red raspberries, elegant with finesse
    Worthy WOTN contender
  • 2020 Château Langoa Barton - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, St. Julien
    UGCB Washington DC tasting:
    Aromatic nose of cherries and barnyard;
    slightly acidic
    Full bodied; dense, dark red fruits
    A young Cali Cab made in a Bordeaux style
    Tasty

Stellar (but expected):
Pichon Lalande
Pichon Baron
Leoville Poyferre
Chateau Clinet

Middle Category:
Chateau Pape Clement (a bit green and firm)

Mildly Disappointing:
Brane Cantenac
Cantenac Brown
Chateau St. Pierre (green)
Chateau Lascombes (tannic, needs time... a lot)

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: Max Swomley
posts.cooked: That Brane Cantenac note hurts - do you remember why it was disappointing?

posts.name: Brian G r a f s t r o m
posts.cooked:
Big time to see Duhart out-performing the Pichons!!

Thanks for the sneak-peek!
posts.display_username: Brian G r a f s t r o m

posts.name: Kevin Patrick
posts.cooked:
First, I would take any reviewer’s notes with a grain of salt. It was one night and I could be a lunatic.

That said, I recall the Brane Cantenac to be a bit brooding and lacking complexity. It was firm and lacked nuance. The fruits were fine but didn’t display much. A great many of the wines showed excellent complexity and finesse – a surprise to me for such a young vintage. On this night the Brane Cantenac was more simple in structure and less interesting than the other wines on the night.

In no way would I write-off this wine. Dip your toe in for a bottle on release and judge for yourself. You are the only true judge of the right wine for you.

Cheers!

posts.name: Ed Steinway
posts.cooked: We had the 2020 Brane Cantenac at the UGC in San Francisco last week and it showed very well, William. As did the Giscours and Cantenac Brown.

posts.name: Jeff Leve
posts.cooked: 2020 Brane Cantenac is IMO, one of the best vintages they’ve ever produced. It is a beautiful wine for my palate.

posts.name: Ry@n S m 1t h
posts.cooked: I couldn’t disagree more with your characterization of the 2020 Brane Cantenac. I didn’t get anything brooding about it. Rather I thought it was extremely fresh with lively acidity and excellent balance.

posts.name: Rhodes Kriete
posts.cooked:
“Chateau Pape Clement (a bit green and firm)”

Never thought I would see that descriptor associated with Pape Clement
